title: Buttery Uruguayan Chickpeas Empanadas
description: Quick-fix, high-protein dish featuring flavorful chickpeas wrapped in buttery pastry.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together flour, salt, and pepper.
Add softened butter and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add 1/4 cup of cold water, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead a few times until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, prepare the chickpea filling by sautéing 1/2 cup of diced onion and 1 minced garlic clove in 2 tablespoons of olive oil until softened.
Add 1 can of chickpeas, drained and rinsed, to the pan and stir to combine.
Add 1 tablespoon of chopped fresh cilantro and 1 teaspoon of ground cumin to the pan and stir to combine.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the chilled dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Use a round cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles of dough.
Place a tablespoon of the chickpea filling in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little water and cut a few slits in the top of each to allow steam to escape.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
